entities.name: SUSTAINABLE FOOD PRODUCTION
entities.description: The goal of producing food in a way that maintains environmental health and resource availability for future generations

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns that challenge food production

entities.name: INCREASING POPULATION
entities.description: The growth of the human population increasing the demand for food

entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE DEGRADATION
entities.description: The deterioration of the environment through depletion of resources

entities.name: SOIL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in soil quality caused by improper use or environmental factors

entities.name: LOSS OF BIODIVERSITY
entities.description: The decrease in the variety of life in a particular habitat or ecosystem

entities.name: CROPPING SYSTEMS
entities.description: The patterns of crops and management practices used on a piece of land

entities.name: CROP DIVERSITY
entities.description: The variety of different crop species grown within a cropping system

entities.name: ANNUAL CROPPING SYSTEMS
entities.description: Systems where crops are grown and harvested within a single year

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: Grass crops grown for their edible seeds

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: Plants of the legume family that can fix nitrogen in the soil

entities.name: OILSEEDS
entities.description: Crops grown primarily for the extraction of oil from their seeds

entities.name: SOIL ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The physical, chemical, and biological characteristics of the soil

entities.name: SOIL ORGANISMS
entities.description: Living organisms found within the soil ecosystem

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The ability to maintain agricultural production over the long term

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The capacity of an agricultural system to recover from disturbances

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process by which legumes convert atmospheric nitrogen into a usable form for plants

entities.name: PEST AND DISEASE CYCLES
entities.description: The recurring patterns of pest and disease outbreaks in crops

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the diverse cropping systems and agroecological patterns are observed

entities.name: MULTIPLE CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops on the same field in a year

entities.name: MONOCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing a single crop species in a field year after year

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: The practice of growing a series of different types of crops in the same area in sequenced seasons

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production harvested per unit area

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients to plant growth

entities.name: NITROGEN FERTILIZER
entities.description: Chemical substances added to soil to provide nitrogen for plant growth

entities.name: DIGITAL FERTILIZER ADVISORY SERVICES
entities.description: Technology-based services providing recommendations on fertilizer application
